dominant_palette: black, white, red/crimson, orange, olive green
secondary_palette: hot pink, bright green, yellow, mid-grey
composition_vocabulary: Overwhelmingly poster-flat and centred — bold type fills the frame, subjects are isolated on stark backgrounds, negative space is used aggressively. Grid-and-column layouts from Swiss/international graphic design sit alongside loose collage. Almost no environmental depth.
lighting_quality: Predominantly flat graphic — the dominant tiles are design artefacts, not photographs; the few photographic tiles (jewellery, torso) use clean studio or ambient natural light with no dramatic shadow play.
material_textures: printed paper, embossed silver leather, flat digital gradient, screen-print halftone, brushed metal (coin-purse tile), raw cardstock
aesthetic_register: The sample lands squarely in contemporary art-school / graphic-design editorial — confrontational, typographic, streetwear-adjacent — with only one or two tiles that read as luxury-adjacent.
notably_absent: There are virtually no close-ups of precious metal surfaces, gemstones, draped fabric, candlelight, or any of the warm heirloom-tactility cues one expects on a luxury-jewellery moodboard.
